skill.name: when-reviewing-github-pr-use-github-code-review
skill.description: Coordinates specialized agents to review GitHub pull requests for security, performance, code quality, tests, and documentation before merge.
skill.summary: Runs a structured multi-agent review process for GitHub pull requests.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static external-command findings are confirmed as security-relevant because the workflow instructs users to execute repo-local shell scripts while reviewing PRs, including external contributor submissions. The weak-cryptography findings appear to be false positives from review terminology, and no prompt injection attempt or confirmed malware was found.

high_findings.title: Repo-local Script Execution During PR Review
high_findings.description: The workflow instructs users to run relative-path shell scripts while reviewing PRs. Because the skill targets external contributor submissions, those paths can resolve to PR-supplied code and create untrusted code execution risk.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands and relative script paths are directly present, and the skill explicitly includes external contributor submissions. This creates a clear path for executing untrusted repository content during review.

medium_findings.title: Unpinned Alpha Package Execution
medium_findings.description: The workflow runs npx claude-flow@alpha, which can download and execute code from a mutable prerelease tag. This is a supply-chain risk even if the package is intended for coordination.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands use npx with an alpha tag, so execution of externally resolved package code is explicit. Legitimate use is possible, but the version is not pinned to an immutable artifact.

medium_findings.title: GitHub Write Actions Lack Permission Guardrails
medium_findings.description: The skill posts reviews, inline comments, and labels to GitHub without documenting token scope, confirmation steps, or dry-run behavior. A compromised review flow could mutate PR state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write operations are explicit and target GitHub PR state. The risk depends on runtime credentials and repository permissions, so confidence is high but not absolute.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported weak-cryptography locations contain review terminology, agent names, hooks, or output descriptions. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, cipher use, or cryptographic implementation was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found no weak cryptographic algorithm usage at these lines. The static matches are consistent with false positives on natural-language review content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Relative Bash Script Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Commands execute scripts under scripts/ using relative paths. During PR review, those files can come from the repository under review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit bash invocations using relative paths. The skill context makes repository-controlled script resolution plausible.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Package Execution With npx
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses npx to run claude-flow@alpha. This executes package code resolved outside the skill package.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The npx commands are directly present. The mutable alpha version increases supply-chain uncertainty.

dangerous_patterns.title: Automated GitHub Mutation Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow includes commands for posting reviews, adding comments, and applying labels to GitHub pull requests.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands explicitly mutate GitHub PR state. Abuse depends on the credentials available at runtime.

content.user_title: Review GitHub Pull Requests With Agents
content.value_statement: Manual PR review often misses cross-cutting issues under time pressure. This skill coordinates specialized review perspectives into one merge readiness assessment.
actual_capabilities: Defines a five-agent mesh review workflow for GitHub pull requests.
Assigns separate review roles for security, performance, quality, tests, and documentation.
Describes how to gather PR context and aggregate agent findings.
Provides weighted merge readiness thresholds for review decisions.
Includes workflows for GitHub review posting, inline comments, labels, and re-review.
limitations: Requires Claude Flow and Flow-Nexus style tools to match the documented workflow.
References helper scripts that are not included in this skill package.
Cannot assess a private repository unless the user provides appropriate access.
Automated fixes and GitHub write actions need human review before use.

use_cases.title: Pre-merge Review Gate
use_cases.description: Run a structured review before maintainers approve a high-impact pull request.

use_cases.title: Security-focused PR Audit
use_cases.description: Coordinate a deeper review of authentication, authorization, injection, secrets, and dependency risks.

use_cases.title: Contributor Feedback Workflow
use_cases.description: Generate organized findings and action items for external or internal contributors.

prompt_templates.title: Basic PR Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this GitHub pull request for security, performance, code quality, tests, and documentation. Summarize merge readiness and required changes.

prompt_templates.title: Security Priority Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review for sensitive code paths.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request with security as the highest priority. Check injection risks, access control, secrets, dependencies, and unsafe data handling.

prompt_templates.title: Large PR Triage
prompt_templates.scenario: Review a broad change set in stages.
prompt_templates.prompt: Triage this large pull request. Identify the highest-risk files first, then provide staged findings for security, performance, tests, and maintainability.

prompt_templates.title: Merge Readiness Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply weighted merge readiness scoring to this pull request. Provide approval status, blocking issues, non-blocking recommendations, and clear action items.

output_examples.input: Review a pull request that changes authentication middleware and tests.
output_examples.output: The review highlights authorization checks, missing negative tests, dependency impact, and a merge decision with required fixes.

output_examples.input: Assess a performance-focused pull request before release.
output_examples.output: The result summarizes algorithmic complexity, database query risks, caching opportunities, test gaps, and recommended optimizations.

output_examples.input: Prepare feedback for an external contributor PR.
output_examples.output: The response groups findings by severity, explains why each issue matters, and lists contributor-ready action items.

best_practices: Review untrusted pull request content before running any repository-local scripts.
Use read-only GitHub credentials until the final review text is approved.
Validate automated findings against project standards before requesting changes.
anti_patterns: Do not execute PR-supplied scripts as part of the first review pass.
Do not post automated review comments without checking them for accuracy.
Do not treat the merge readiness score as a replacement for maintainer judgment.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews security, performance, code quality, tests, documentation, and merge readiness for GitHub pull requests.

faq.question: Does it require GitHub access?
faq.answer: Yes. It needs repository and pull request context, and write access only if posting comments or labels.

faq.question: Can it approve pull requests automatically?
faq.answer: It can describe an approval decision, but a human maintainer should confirm before posting or merging.

faq.question: Are helper scripts included?
faq.answer: No. The skill references helper scripts, but this package only contains the skill document and process diagram.

faq.question: Is it safe for external contributor PRs?
faq.answer: Use caution. Do not run repository-local scripts from an untrusted PR without isolation and review.

faq.question: Which tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code, with the documented workflow centered on Claude Code style tasks.
